summary: buffer-util-internal performs import-time remote code execution via base64-hidden jsonkeeper.com loader
details: On require/import, index.js decodes a base64-obfuscated URL (resolving to https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/CWOV9), fetches JSON from that anonymous paste host, and passes the response's `content` field to `eval`. This gives whoever controls the paste arbitrary code execution on any machine that installs or imports the package. A second dormant base64 URL (https://www.jsonkeeper.com/b/PT0ON) is also embedded. The package additionally impersonates the legitimate `buffer` package by copying its README, author (Feross Aboukhadijeh), and repository metadata, and declares unused decoy dependencies (axios, request, execp, fs, path). The combination of typosquat metadata, base64-obfuscated loader URLs, an anonymous mutable paste service, and unconditional eval of fetched content at module load is a malicious-loader supply-chain attack.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
